Code fragment: public final TableField<ContinentRecord, String> CONTINENT_NAME = createField(DSL.name("continent_name"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR(255), this, "");
Explanation: The CONTINENT_NAME column is marked as NULLABLE (no `.nullable(false)` specified), but a continent name should never be NULL in practice as every continent must have a name. Consider adding a NOT NULL constraint to this column.

Code fragment: public final TableField<MemberRecord, Long> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key column is named just 'id', which is a generic name that doesn't convey any meaning about the entity it identifies. Consider renaming it to 'member_id' to make it more descriptive and to avoid ambiguity in queries with joins.

Code fragment: public final TableField<OrganizationRecord, Long> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
